title.purpose: Evidence section headings and compact surface headings.

body.purpose: Data cells, helper copy, and review details.

label.purpose: Table headers, eyebrows, and definition-list labels.

motion.name: state-fast
motion.purpose: Simple hover, focus, and color state changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Use for the next operational action, such as uploading a statement.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { min-height: 44px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border: 0; border-radius: 6px; background: var(--accent); color: var(--accent-foreground); padding: 0 16px; font: 600 14px/1.2 Geist, Arial, sans-serif; transition: opacity 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Use for adjacent navigation or settings checks.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { min-height: 44px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; border: 1px solid var(--border-subtle); border-radius: 6px; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); padding: 0 16px; font: 500 14px/1.2 Geist, Arial, sans-serif; transition: background 150ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: var(--surface-muted); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ready State Badge
components.description: Status badge for persisted document states.
components.css: .ds-state-badge { min-height: 28px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0 10px; font: 600 12px/1 Geist, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-state-ready { background: color-mix(in oklch, var(--success) 16%, transparent); color: var(--success); } .ds-state-badge:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Evidence Panel
components.description: Bordered section for document, statement, audit, or deletion evidence.
components.css: .ds-evidence-panel { border: 1px solid var(--border-subtle); border-radius: 8px; background: var(--background); color: var(--foreground); padding: 20px; font-family: Geist, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-evidence-panel h2 { margin: 0 0 16px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.35; font-weight: 600; } .ds-evidence-panel dl { display: grid; gap: 12px; margin: 0; font-size: 14px; } .ds-evidence-panel dt { color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--foreground) 50%, transparent); } .ds-evidence-panel dd { margin: 2px 0 0; font-weight: 500; overflow-wrap: anywhere; }

components.name: History Table
components.description: Dense review table for document state, statement evidence, audit event, and retention proof.
components.css: .ds-history-table { width: 100%; min-width: 720px; border: 1px solid var(--border-subtle); border-radius: 8px; border-collapse: separate; border-spacing: 0; overflow: hidden; color: var(--foreground); font: 14px/1.5 Geist, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-history-table thead { background: var(--surface-muted); color: color-mix(in oklch, var(--foreground) 45%, transparent); font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-history-table th, .ds-history-table td { padding: 12px 16px; text-align: left; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-subtle); } .ds-history-table tbody tr:last-child td { border-bottom: 0; }

narrative.overview: PRIZM is a calm product interface for financial operators who need to see what happened, when it happened, and whether it is safe to act. The visual system is restrained by design: pale teal-tinted neutrals, a single operational accent, compact tables, and direct labels that make status and evidence inspectable.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained, evidence-first product UI with no ornamental trust language.
Light by default for accounting work in ordinary office conditions, with dark-mode tokens available through the same roles.
Tables, definition lists, and bordered sections carry most evidence surfaces.
Accent color is rare and functional: primary actions, links, focus rings, and active evidence.

rules.name: The Evidence Color Rule
rules.body: Color never carries meaning alone. Every status color must be paired with text such as Pending, Ready, Failed, Expired, Receipt sent, or Trace.

rules.name: The Rare Accent Rule
rules.body: Audit Teal is functional, never decorative. Do not use it as a hero wash, background flourish, gradient, or large brand field.

rules.name: The Flat Evidence Rule
rules.body: Use borders and background roles before shadows. Shadows are reserved for future overlays or menus, not default panels.

rules.name: The No Floating Card Rule
rules.body: Page sections are not decorative cards. Use bordered evidence sections, tables, and definition lists; avoid nested cards.

dos: Do pair every trust claim with an implementation artifact: state, receipt, timestamp, request ID, trace ID, retention deadline, or audit event.
Do use the five persisted document labels exactly: Pending, Processing, Ready, Failed, Expired.
Do keep PRIZM dense and scannable. Tables and definition lists are the default evidence surfaces.
Do keep Accent usage rare and functional.
Do preserve workspace and audit language in the UI whenever data crosses a sensitive boundary.
donts: Don't use generic SaaS gloss, vague trust copy, decorative security badges, gradient theater, or claims that sound confident without showing evidence.
Don't use consumer fintech flash, neon dashboards, gamified money visuals, crypto-adjacent styling, or unserious animation.
Don't bury compliance in policy-only copy while the workflow stays opaque.
Don't use side-stripe borders, gradient text, glassmorphism, hero metrics, or identical decorative card grids.
Don't communicate status by color alone.
